Verified using Mohr's Circle formula: P1 = (80 + (-40))/2 + sqrt(((80 - (-40))/2)^2 + 25^2) = 20 + 65 = 85. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mohr's_circle
Verified using Mohr's Circle formula: P2 = (80 + (-40))/2 - sqrt(((80 - (-40))/2)^2 + 25^2) = 20 - 65 = -45. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mohr's_circle
Used in verifier.py lines 34 and 101 for matching principal stresses

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task for creating a Mohr's Circle stress analysis visualization in GeoGebra. The task requires setting three sliders (sig_x=80, sig_y=-40, tau=25) and constructing a circle whose intersections with the x-axis represent the principal stresses. The principal stress values have been mathematically verified: P1=85 and P2=-45 are derived from the standard Mohr's Circle formula using the target slider values. All metadata values are consistent with the task description and engineering mechanics principles.
